title: Alan Greenspan, former Federal Reserve Chairman, dies at 100
summary: The legendary central banker, known as 'The Maestro', led US monetary policy for over 18 years.

title: British Prime Minister Starmer announces resignation amid internal pressures
summary: Starmer's departure comes after loss of support in his party and the advancement of rival figures, generating political uncertainty.

title: Mutilated body found in freezer in Kobe apartment
summary: The body, which dates from approximately 2011, belonged to a former resident and was discovered after a complaint about a bad odor.

title: Author of murder of student in Asahikawa sentenced to 27 years in prison
summary: The court considered that the accused acted with extreme cruelty and contempt for life, in a case that shocked the country.

title: Progress in nuclear negotiations between US and Iran
summary: Iran accepts nuclear inspections in exchange for oil sales, as high-level talks on regional security continue.

title: Japan and US finance ministers hold urgent talks on exchange rate
summary: The virtual meeting addressed yen volatility and possible currency intervention measures to stabilize markets.

title: Progress in reform of Imperial House Law to ensure succession
summary: The government and parliamentary leaders agree to allow adoption of males to maintain the male dynastic line.

title: Japanese national team prepares for World Cup round of 16
summary: Japan analyzes potential rivals and optimizes offensive performance after a group stage with high scoring efficiency.

title: Yen falls to 39-year low against dollar
summary: The Japanese currency weakens to 161 yen per dollar, fueling expectations of state intervention.

title: Explosion at world's largest LNG plant in Qatar leaves dozens dead and injured
summary: The deflagration in the north of the country caused at least 13 deaths and more than 50 injuries.

title: JR Central to operate first-ever nighttime bullet train in August
summary: The special 'Night Shinkansen' service will leave Tokyo for Osaka to offer an economical and efficient option for travelers.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi requests removal of referendum clause in 'subcapital' project
summary: Takaichi and Osaka Governor Yoshimura negotiate changes to the administrative decentralization initiative.

title: Aung San Suu Kyi's dog dies without her being able to say goodbye
summary: The dog died while the Burmese leader remains under house arrest after the coup.

title: Japanese police order detectives not to disclose information to suspected stalkers
summary: For the first time, police request a detective agency to stop providing data that could facilitate stalking.

title: Fair Trade Commission sanctions Kawai for paying excessively low wages to instructors
summary: It was determined that the company engaged in 'low-price purchasing' by setting remuneration far below the value of the service.

title: Miyuu Yamashita wins first tournament of the season on LPGA Tour
summary: The Japanese golfer came back in the playoff to take the victory and climb in the world rankings.

title: 81st anniversary of Battle of Okinawa and legacy of suffering persists
summary: Through social media and commemorative events, new generations keep alive the memory of the 200,000 dead in the conflict.

title: Argentina beats Austria and Messi reaches 18 World Cup goals
summary: The Argentine national team prevailed in the Group J match with an outstanding performance by its captain.

title: Egypt gets first World Cup victory after 92-year wait
summary: A goal by Mohamed Salah and the team's fighting spirit gave victory over New Zealand in a thrilling match.

title: Emperors of Japan begin state visit to Belgium
summary: Naruhito and Masako met with King Philippe and Queen Mathilde at the Castle of Laeken, strengthening bilateral ties.
